system.name: Open SIR Epidemic
system.description: The OPEN counterpart to sir-epidemic: identical compartment topology (S->I->R with non-draining I-Sensor control), but Recovered drains to an external Mortality sink across the system boundary. Because mass crosses the boundary, the model is NOT closed: internal mass (compartment storage + internal in-flight) is no longer invariant, but obeys the exact open-system balance internal(t) = internal(0) - cumulative deaths. Contrast with sir-epidemic.json (closed, total mass conserved to machine epsilon). Demonstrates BERT reasoning about open vs. closed systems. Synchronous update mode; seed storages via initial_state (S=100, I=5, R=0).

sinks.name: Mortality
sinks.description: External sink: recovered individuals exit the population (death). The boundary outflow that makes this system open.

subsystems.name: Susceptible
subsystems.description: Susceptible population stock; drained by infection

subsystems.name: Infected
subsystems.description: Infected population stock; gains from infection, loses to recovery

subsystems.name: Recovered
subsystems.description: Recovered population stock; gains from recovery, drains across the boundary to Mortality

subsystems.name: I-Sensor
subsystems.description: Senses the Infected level (non-draining) and emits the infection-rate control signal
